name: ICM Poojappura

name: TKM College of Engineering,Kollam
description: TKM College of Engineering, located in Karicode, Kollam, Kerala, is one of the oldest and most reputed engineering institutions in the state, established in 1958. The college is a government-aided autonomous institution affiliated with APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University and approved by AICTE, with NAAC ‘A’ grade accreditation. It offers a wide range of undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs in engineering, architecture, and computer applications. Known for its strong academic foundation, experienced faculty, modern infrastructure, and active placement cell, TKMCE has consistently produced skilled engineers and professionals. The campus provides excellent facilities including well-equipped laboratories, libraries, hostels, and research centers, fostering innovation and holistic student development.
financials:
B.Tech (Merit): ~₹8,500 – ₹10,000

B.Tech (Management): ~₹65,000 – ₹1,00,000 (Varies by branch)

M.Tech: ~₹16,000 – ₹32,000 (Self-financing streams are higher)

MCA: ~₹12,000 – ₹15,000
campus_student_life: Student life at TKMCE in 2026 is a high-octane blend of "Mysore-style" architectural grandeur and a legendary, student-driven culture. Life here revolves around Hestia, South India's largest techno-cultural fest, where the campus transforms into a hub of international "Pro-Nites" and intense technical competitions like Conjura and Celestra. Beyond the fests, daily life is anchored by a mandatory multi-club system (students typically join 2–4 clubs), ranging from the high-tech C-Rob (Robotics) and SAE India to the creative Music, Dance, and Film clubs. While the urban 25-acre campus is cozy and sometimes congested, it fosters a tight-knit community where students bond at the "Front Square" or local "thattukadas" in Karicode. Despite a rigorous academic schedule and early-year hostel curfews, the absence of a rigid uniform and a thriving, ragging-free atmosphere make TKMCE one of the most vibrant and socially active engineering destinations in Kerala.
immigration_support:
For international students or those under the NRI/Foreign National quota at TKM College of Engineering (TKMCE), immigration and stay-related compliance are managed through the Office of International Collaboration and the Dean of Student Affairs.

For matters involving Visa processing, Bonafide Certificates, and Foreign Regional Registration Office (FRRO) documentation

name: St. Xavier's College, India

Affiliated with the University of Kerala and accredited with an 'A' grade by NAAC, the college offers a range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs in Arts, Science, and Commerce. It is renowned for its academic excellence, social commitment, and holistic approach to education. The campus provides a culturally rich environment with a strong emphasis on discipline and moral values, making it an ideal destination for students seeking quality education in India.
campus_student_life: St. Xavier's College, India offers an enriching campus experience on its 110-acre site near the Thumba coastline. The college features modern infrastructure including smart classrooms, advanced science labs, and a multi-disciplinary library. Students engage in various clubs, cultural fests, and sports, promoting holistic growth in a safe and supportive Jesuit environment. The serene coastal setting and proximity to major research centers provide a unique backdrop for academic and personal development.
immigration_support: The college provides support for Student Visa (S-1) documentation and assistance with mandatory FRRO registration for international students.

name: Mar Ivanios College - Thiruvananthapuram
description: Mar Ivanios College, established in 1949, is a premier educational institution in Kerala, India. It was the first institution in the University of Kerala to receive autonomous status. Accredited by NAAC with an 'A++' grade, the college is recognized as a 'College with Potential for Excellence' by the UGC. It offers a diverse range of undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs in Arts, Science, and Commerce, focusing on academic rigor, character building, and social commitment.
financials:

Self-Financing Courses (B.Com/BCA/BBA): ₹30,000 – ₹60,000 ($360 – $720) per year.

Master's Programs: ₹10,000 – ₹80,000 ($120 – $960) depending on the stream.

Fees are lower compared to private institutions due to government subsidies.
campus_student_life: Mar Ivanios College fosters a vibrant student life through its 40-acre lush green "Vidya Nagar" campus. The institution operates on a value-based educational model with active student councils and numerous clubs like NCC, NSS, and Fine Arts Society. The campus is equipped with smart classrooms, advanced science labs, a multi-story library, and high-quality sports infrastructure (including a stadium). It is famous for its "Ivanios Fest" and cultural dominance in university youth festivals.
immigration_support: As an autonomous college under the University of Kerala, the International Admission Cell facilitates Bonafide Certificates and supporting documents for foreign nationals. name: Saint Martin's Institute
description: Established in 1985, Saint Martin’s Institute of Higher Education is a leading private tertiary institution in Malta, renowned for its commitment to academic excellence and student-centered learning. Licensed by the Malta Further & Higher Education Authority (MFHEA), the institute provides a high-quality alternative to the national university, offering a range of "home-grown" and internationally recognized qualifications.

Unlike massive, impersonal universities, SMI operates as a close-knit community where students have direct access to faculty and are encouraged to take an active role in campus life. The institution provides high-tech smart classrooms, specialized computing and AI labs, and dedicated study zones, creating a seamless blend between rigorous academic training and professional development.

name: LEAD College
description: LEAD College of Management (Autonomous) is a premier residential business school specializing in leadership and entrepreneurship. As a pioneer in experiential management education in India, LEAD has redefined the traditional MBA by blending academic rigor with a "Learning by Doing" philosophy. Located at the serene foothills of Dhoni, Palakkad, the institution provides an immersive environment designed to transform ambitious students into socially committed corporate leaders.
financials:
Master (MBA) fee: ₹1,80,000 / semester ($1,950 / semester) — 4 semesters

Master (MCA) fee: ₹1,35,000 / semester ($1,460 / semester) — 4 semesters

The MBA fee is a comprehensive package. It includes tuition, air-conditioned accommodation, all meals, uniforms, laptops, textbooks, and international training modules.
campus_student_life: LEAD’s campus fosters a vibrant student life through an immersive 24/7 residential model, active community involvement, and student-led governance. Unlike traditional institutions, LEAD operates as a "living laboratory" where students manage the college's daily operations through the LEAD Operating Team (LOT), gaining direct experience in administration, event management, and corporate relations. The institution provides modern, fully-equipped residential facilities, high-tech smart classrooms, and extensive libraries, ensuring a seamless blend of academic and personal life.
immigration_support: LEAD College provides comprehensive immigration assistance through its International Admission Cell, specifically designed for students from over 10 nations. The institution facilitates the issuance of official Admission Offer Letters and Bonafide Certificates required for the Student Visa (S-1) process. Upon arrival, the administrative team provides end-to-end support for mandatory registration with the Foreigners Regional Registration Office (FRRO) and helps students obtain the necessary AIU (Association of Indian Universities) equivalency certificates for their foreign qualifications.

It's known for industry-leading knowledge, hands-on training (like flight programs), and partnerships with major aerospace companies, producing graduates for aviation, aerospace, and related tech fields. ERAU is often called the "Harvard of the sky" for its deep focus on aeronautics and strong reputation.

Students experience a unique culture with aviation-focused events, numerous clubs (film, gaming, international), and a mix of social activities, all within a unique environment focused on future leaders in aviation and beyond, with mandatory first two years of on-campus housing in Daytona Beach.

name: EBS University of Business and Law
description: EBS University of Business and Law is a private university specializing in business administration and law. As a pioneer among German business schools, EBS is once again setting new standards 40 years after its founding: with its expansion from a business school into EBS University, it has further evolved and now also helps aspiring lawyers climb the career ladder. With many years of experience, an outstanding range of courses, and a committed student body, EBS offers the perfect environment for ambitious individuals. We prepare you for a career both in Germany and abroad – through strong practical orientation, excellent teaching, optimal study conditions, an international outlook, and the targeted development of your personality.
campus_student_life:
Student life is more than just hours spent in lecture halls; it’s a journey of self-discovery and community building. We have created a diverse environment for learning and living, where every day is filled with energy and new experiences.

Our campus is a place where you can find everything you need: from modern labs and well-stocked libraries to cozy cafes and lush green spaces for relaxation. The most special part, however, is our wide range of extracurricular activities, including academic clubs, sports teams, arts groups, and community volunteer projects. This is a fantastic opportunity for you to develop soft skills, expand your network, and find your passion.

Imagine collaborating with friends on a startup project, rehearsing for a music performance, or simply cheering on your school’s sports team. These moments will become unforgettable memories, serving as valuable preparation for your future.

Come and join us in writing the most brilliant and meaningful chapter of your youth!

Their academic programs are extensive, ranging from doctoral degrees such as the Doctorate in Business Administration (DBA), Master's degrees like the Master of Business Administration (MBA), to Bachelor's programs (BBA, BSC), Diplomas, and various professional certifications. The institution prides itself on impressive graduate outcomes, with 92% of its graduates finding employment within 6 months and 85% reporting an improvement in their income after completing their studies.

name: Alfred State College
description:
Alfred State College is a renowned public college within the State University of New York (SUNY) system, located in Alfred, New York. The school is known for its "hands-on learning" approach and a strong focus on vocational, technical, and technological programs.
With a small community feel, Alfred State offers a friendly and highly supportive learning environment. Students benefit from direct access to faculty and modern laboratories and workshops. The diverse range of academic programs, from architecture, construction, and engineering to health sciences, agriculture, and business administration, are all designed to equip students with the necessary skills for success upon graduation. The college also boasts an impressive job placement and further education rate, confirming the quality of its training and the relevance of its programs to the needs of the job market.
campus_student_life: Alfred State College offers a well-rounded student experience, emphasizing hands-on learning within the SUNY system, supported by over 90 clubs, 17 intercollegiate sports teams, and a strong focus on student leadership and civic engagement. name: IMT Atlantique
description:
IMT Atlantique is a leading general engineering school and a research center. It is made up of three campuses: Brest, Nantes, and Rennes. IMT Atlantique is part of the Institut Mines-Télécom and a member of the Université Bretagne Loire.
IMT Atlantique offers a unique general engineering degree. Recruitment of students from preparatory classes for the grandes écoles is carried out through the common Mines-Ponts entrance exam.
The school also offers apprenticeship-based engineering programs, master’s degrees, Master of Science programs, Erasmus Mundus programs, specialized master’s degrees, and doctoral training.
IMT Atlantique combines expertise in digital technology, energy, and the environment.
campus_student_life: Student life at IMT Atlantique is diverse, with a wide range of extracurricular, sports, and cultural activities across its Brest, Nantes, and Rennes campuses. Students can join more than 100 clubs and associations, coordinated by the IMT Atlantique Student Union, which organizes events such as parties, trips, and activities related to music, dance, cooking, and more. In addition, the school offers high-quality sports facilities, including sports fields, gyms, as well as resources like a Fablab and an observatory.

name: Liberty University
description: Liberty University in Lynchburg, Virginia (USA) is one of the largest private Christian universities in the world, founded in 1971. With more than 700 academic programs and a strong focus on online education, the university attracts over 140,000 students from all 50 U.S. states and more than 100 countries. Its 7,000-acre campus combines modern facilities ranging from research, medical, and arts centers to NCAA Division I athletic complexes, offering a comprehensive environment for learning, research, and personal development. Liberty strives to equip students with knowledge, skills, and global values within a vibrant, diverse community grounded in Christian spirit.

campus_student_life: Campus & Student Life at Liberty University takes place on a 7,000-acre campus in Lynchburg, Virginia, featuring modern academic buildings, the Jerry Falwell Library, Freedom Tower, and specialized laboratories. Students under 21 who are unmarried typically live in residence halls, with options ranging from shared rooms to apartment-style housing with private kitchens. Student life is vibrant with over 40 club sports, 20 NCAA Division I teams, and annual events such as Welcome Week, Block Party, concerts, and arts activities. Beyond academics, students enjoy unique facilities like the Snowflex year-round ski slope, fitness centers, and state-of-the-art athletic venues. The university’s culture is rooted in Christian values, with the Office of Student Life providing spiritual, community, and personal support, while upholding lifestyle standards for residential students.
immigration_support: The international student center provides support for students here on student (f1) status, which is a non-immigrant visa. The student center also supports students who wish to work in the us following graduation on optional practical training (OPT), which allows students to work for one year (or longer for stem majors) in an area related to their major. The university does not support the immigration process as that takes place long after the student is no longer on ‘student’ (f1) status in the us.  Immigration lawyers in the US can guide students through the process of applying for h-1b or legal permanent residency as needed.
